description:
Goal: make `dispatch_matrix` a shared compiler service that Compiler2 can consume directly without copying or old-world handle leakage.

Change:
- Neutralize the source-pattern producer boundary so it no longer depends on `fz_ir::Var`-flavored input identity in shared planner data.
- Replace old-world input/precondition handles with producer-neutral ordinals or equivalent shared input refs, while keeping `PatternDispatchPlan` AST-free and consumer-neutral.
- Update old lowering, interp, codegen, and frontend pattern-check consumers at their edges instead of forking planner logic into Compiler2.
- Preserve existing decision-graph, evidence/projection, pin, guard, map, bitstring, and type-order semantics.

Why:
- Copying `dispatch_matrix` and its tests into Compiler2 would fork semantics and create two matchers to keep in sync.
- The current coupling is shallow and local; paying it off once is cheaper and cleaner than a long-lived duplicate.

Checks:
- Existing `dispatch_matrix` tests stay green with no semantic drift.
- Existing old-world consumers still compile and pass with the neutralized planner boundary.
- No Compiler2 semantic jobs depend on a copied planner tree.

Non-goals:
- No Compiler2-specific dispatch facts yet.
- No change to the core dispatch algorithm beyond representational neutralization.
title: Compiler2 dispatch: neutralize shared dispatch_matrix for cross-compiler reuse
